passages.text: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST), launched in late 2021, represents a significant leap forward in our ability to observe the cosmos. Unlike its predecessor, the Hubble Space Telescope, which primarily observes visible light, the JWST is designed to detect infrared radiation. This capability allows it to peer through dense clouds of gas and dust that often obscure the formation of stars and planetary systems. Furthermore, because the light from the most distant objects in the universe is 'redshifted' into the infrared spectrum by the expansion of space, the JWST can observe the very first stars and galaxies that formed after the Big Bang. This provides astronomers with an unprecedented window into the early history of the universe.

The Great Depression of the 1930s was the most severe economic downturn in the history of the industrialized world. Triggered by the 1929 stock market crash, it led to widespread unemployment, poverty, and a massive decline in global trade. In response to the crisis, many countries implemented protectionist trade policies, such as the Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act in the United States, which raised duties on imported goods. However, these measures backfired as other nations retaliated with their own tariffs, leading to a collapse in international commerce. It was not until the onset of World War II that increased government spending on defense began to pull the global economy out of the depression.

questions.question: Kuantum mekaniği, atom altı parçacıkların davranışlarını yöneten fizik yasalarını anlamamıza olanak tanıyarak modern teknolojinin gelişiminde hayati bir rol oynamıştır.

options.A: Quantum mechanics has played a vital role in the development of modern technology by allowing us to understand the laws of physics governing the behavior of subatomic particles.
options.B: The role of quantum mechanics in modern technology is vital because it explains how subatomic particles behave under physical laws.
options.C: By understanding the behavior of subatomic particles, quantum mechanics has contributed to the development of many technologies in the modern world.
options.D: Modern technology owes its development to quantum mechanics, which explores the laws of physics regarding subatomic particles.
options.E: It was through the behavior of subatomic particles that quantum mechanics became a vital part of the development of modern technology.

questions.question: Although many experts predicted a sharp decline in the housing market, the actual data suggests a steady increase in property values over the last quarter.

options.A: Birçok uzman konut piyasasında keskin bir düşüş öngörmesine rağmen, güncel veriler son çeyrekte mülk değerlerinde istikrarlı bir artış olduğunu göstermektedir.
options.B: Pek çok uzman konut piyasasının çökeceğini düşünse de, veriler mülklerin değerinin son çeyrekte arttığını kanıtlamıştır.
options.C: Konut piyasasında görülen keskin düşüş beklentisi, son çeyrek verilerinin mülk değerlerinde artış göstermesiyle boşa çıkmıştır.
options.D: Son çeyrekteki mülk değerleri, uzmanların konut piyasasında beklediği düşüşün aksine istikrarlı bir şekilde yükselmiştir.
options.E: Veriler mülk değerlerinin son çeyrekte arttığını gösterse bile, uzmanlar konut piyasasında ciddi bir düşüş olacağı konusunda hemfikirdi.

questions.question: Yapay zeka sistemleri geliştikçe, etik standartların belirlenmesi ve veri gizliliğinin korunması daha kritik bir hale gelmektedir.

options.D: The more artificial intelligence systems develop, the more critical it is to set ethical rules and ensure data privacy.
options.E: Establishing ethical standards and protecting data privacy becomes critical only after artificial intelligence systems have developed.

questions.question: Despite being one of the most widely spoken languages in the world, Mandarin Chinese is often perceived as one of the most difficult to learn for native English speakers.

options.A: Dünyada en yaygın konuşulan dillerden biri olmasına rağmen, Mandarin Çincesi genellikle ana dili İngilizce olanlar için öğrenilmesi en zor dillerden biri olarak algılanır.
options.B: Mandarin Çincesi dünyadaki en yaygın dillerden biri olsa da, İngilizler için bu dili öğrenmek oldukça zordur.
options.C: Ana dili İngilizce olanlar Mandarin Çincesini zor bulur; oysa bu dil dünyada en çok konuşulan diller arasındadır.
options.D: Mandarin Çincesinin dünyada çok yaygın olması, ana dili İngilizce olanların bu dili öğrenmesini kolaylaştırmaz.
options.E: Dünyanın en çok konuşulan dili olduğu halde Mandarin Çincesini öğrenmek, İngilizce bilenler için imkansız kabul edilir.

questions.question: Only by analyzing the fossil records can scientists reconstruct the evolutionary history of these extinct species.

options.D: Reconstructing the history of extinct species requires scientists to find more fossils than they already have.
options.E: Scientists analyzed the fossil records to understand why these species became extinct.

questions.question: Hardly anyone expected the candidate to win the election with such a significant margin.

options.E: The candidate won the election because everyone expected him to win with a huge margin.

questions.question: If the government had taken earlier action, the economic crisis could have been avoided.

options.E: As the government did not take any action, the economic crisis is still continuing today.

questions.question:
Professor: Your research paper on renewable energy sources is quite comprehensive, but you seem to have overlooked the cost-benefit analysis of tidal power.
Student: --- 
Professor: Indeed. Adding a section on that would strengthen your argument significantly.

options.A: I believe tidal power is not as efficient as wind energy, so I excluded it.
options.B: I actually thought about it, but I wasn't sure if enough data was available.
options.C: Is tidal power really that important for the current energy market?
options.D: I will definitely look into that and incorporate it into my final draft.
options.E: I had trouble finding the right resources to explain tidal energy's mechanics.

questions.question:
Doctor: The tests show that your blood pressure is consistently high, which could lead to serious health issues.
Patient: I've been under a lot of stress lately at work. Could that be the reason?
Doctor: --- 
Patient: I'll try to find some relaxation techniques then.

options.A: Stress is a common factor, but we should also examine your diet and exercise habits.
options.B: No, stress usually has no impact on physical health indicators like blood pressure.
options.C: It is impossible to tell without further psychological evaluation.
options.D: You should consider quitting your job to improve your health immediately.
options.E: High blood pressure is mostly genetic, so stress is probably not the main cause.

questions.question:
Interviewer: How do you handle conflict within a team environment?
Applicant: I believe communication is the key. I try to listen to all perspectives before reaching a compromise.
Interviewer: --- 
Applicant: Certainly. In my previous role, I mediated a disagreement between the design and marketing teams regarding a project deadline.

options.A: That sounds good, but what if a compromise cannot be reached?
options.B: Can you provide a specific example of when you did this?
options.C: Why do you think communication is so important in a team?
options.D: Have you ever failed to resolve a conflict in your career?
options.E: Do you prefer working alone or as part of a large team?

questions.question:
Librarian: I'm sorry, but that particular book has already been checked out by another student.
Student: That's a shame. I really need it for my thesis which is due next week.
Librarian: --- 
Student: That would be great! Please let me know as soon as it's back.

options.A: You should have come earlier to borrow it.
options.B: We have an e-book version available that you can access right now.
options.C: I can place a hold on it for you, and you'll be notified once it's returned.
options.D: Maybe you can change the topic of your thesis to something else.
options.E: The book is expected to be returned in three weeks, which is too late for you.

questions.question: Nutrition plays a fundamental role in maintaining physical health and cognitive function. A balanced diet provides the necessary vitamins and minerals for the body to perform its daily tasks. ---. For instance, diets high in processed sugars have been linked to an increased risk of depression and anxiety.

options.A: Moreover, physical exercise is equally important for mental well-being.
options.B: However, many people still prefer fast food due to their busy schedules.
options.C: Consequently, nutritional deficiencies can lead to various chronic diseases.
options.D: In addition to physical health, what we eat also significantly impacts our mental state.

questions.question: The Industrial Revolution marked a major turning point in history, transitioning societies from agrarian economies to manufacturing powerhouses. This period saw the rise of factories and urban centers. ---. Working conditions in early factories were often hazardous, and child labor was common until labor laws were eventually introduced.

options.A: Technological advancements like the steam engine made this transition possible.
options.B: Yet, this rapid industrialization also brought about significant social challenges.
options.C: Most people moved to cities in search of better-paying jobs in these factories.
options.D: The growth of the middle class was a direct result of the industrial boom.
options.E: Steam engines replaced animal power, increasing production efficiency.

questions.question: Urban planning is essential for creating sustainable and livable cities. It involves the design of transportation systems, public spaces, and housing. ---. Without proper planning, cities often face issues like traffic congestion, pollution, and inadequate infrastructure.

options.A: Effective planning ensures that resources are allocated efficiently to meet the needs of citizens.
options.B: Architects are often tasked with designing the most iconic buildings in the city.
options.C: Many people prefer living in the suburbs rather than the crowded city center.
options.D: The history of urban planning dates back to ancient civilizations like Rome.
options.E: Public transportation is the most important aspect of any modern city's design.

questions.question: Quantum computing represents the next frontier in technology, promising to solve problems that are currently impossible for classical computers. Unlike classical bits, which are either 0 or 1, qubits can exist in multiple states simultaneously. ---. This allows quantum computers to process vast amounts of data at unprecedented speeds.

options.A: This phenomenon is known as superposition, a core principle of quantum mechanics.
options.B: Classical computers are still more reliable for everyday tasks like word processing.
options.C: The development of quantum hardware is currently a very expensive endeavor.
options.D: Many tech companies are investing billions of dollars into quantum research.
options.E: Cybersecurity will be significantly impacted by the advent of quantum machines.

questions.question: Why does the JWST observe infrared radiation instead of visible light?

options.A: Visible light is too dangerous for the telescope's sensitive instruments.
options.B: Infrared radiation is easier to transmit back to Earth.
options.C: It enables the telescope to see through cosmic dust and gas clouds.
options.D: The Hubble Telescope already captured all possible visible light images.
options.E: Infrared radiation is the only type of light present in our solar system.

questions.question: What does the term 'redshifted' imply in the context of the passage?

options.A: The objects are becoming hotter and emitting more red light.
options.B: The light's wavelength is stretched due to the expansion of the universe.
options.C: The objects are physically moving closer to the telescope.
options.D: The telescope's lens is tinted red to enhance image quality.
options.E: The light is being reflected off the surface of red dwarf stars.

questions.question: According to the passage, how did the Great Depression affect international trade?

options.A: It encouraged countries to lower tariffs to boost global consumption.
options.B: It led many nations to adopt protectionist policies, worsening the crisis.
options.C: It had no significant impact on trade outside of the United States.
options.D: It forced countries to switch from the gold standard to digital currency.
options.E: It created a global boom in the manufacturing of luxury goods.

questions.question: What was the unintended consequence of the Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act?

options.A: It made American goods cheaper for foreign consumers.
options.B: It caused other nations to impose their own tariffs in response.
options.C: It immediately solved the unemployment problem in the US.
options.D: It led to a surplus of imported goods in the American market.
options.E: It stabilized the stock market within a few months of its passage.

questions.question: Which of the following can be inferred from the passage about the end of the Great Depression?

options.A: It was primarily ended by the natural recovery of the stock market.
options.B: Protective tariffs were the most effective tool in ending the crisis.
options.C: Military spending during World War II played a role in the economic recovery.
options.D: The global economy recovered as soon as international trade was banned.
options.E: The depression would have ended much sooner if the gold standard was kept.
